meaning: <西>n.(加那利群岛中戈梅拉岛土著居民使用的)哨语[字面意义:whistle,whistling]
examples: Spanish Silbo, a whistle language, has only four vowel and four consonant sounds. Audible for miles, it resembles bird calls.
At 39 years old, Isidro is one of the few young people who learned silbo gomero directly from his parent.
The whistled language of La Gomera Island in the Canaries, the Silbo Gomero, replicates the islanders' habitual language (Castilian Spanish) with whistling.
Taught in schools since 1999, the Silbo Gomero is understood by almost all islanders and practised by the vast majority, particularly the elderly and the young.
